channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Household furniture, 7th April
item.description:
A sofa right by the communal bins

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Lockhurst Street, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Illegal parking and obstruction, 7th April
item.description:
Illegal parking and obstruction

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Lower Maidstone Road, Southgate
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Outside 273 Kilburn Lane, 6th April
item.description:
Pavement has a large deep hole, unclear whether it’s abandoned Utility work or what. No signage, barriers all over the ground, blocking Road and pavement but not preventing anyone falling in the hole as they have mostly collapsed. Hole has been there more than a week with no action at all.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kilburn Lane, Kilburn, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large pothole opposite golf club entrance, 6th April
item.description:
There is a large pothole in the outside lane of the northbound carriageway of the A12 near to the turning for Maylands golf club. It is large enough to cause a serious danger to any two wheeled vehicles with the potential that if theu hit it they could come off and cause serious injury to themselves.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Colchester Road, Harold Wood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large 2 foot +pothole, 6th April
item.description:
The pothole is very large in the middle of the road
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Crewys Road, Nunhead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Keeps going to red, 6th April
item.description:
Keeps going to red when there is none there.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Shooters Hill, Shooters Hill, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Multiple dangerous, deep potholes causing vehicle damage, 6th April
item.description:
There are a number of deep potholes along Oakleigh Road North (heading towards Southgate, near Tesco), and they’re directly in the normal driving path so they’re very difficult to avoid. I’ve already had tyre damage from hitting them, and they seem to be getting worse over time. It’s not just one pothole either, it’s a stretch of the road that’s breaking up. I believe this has been reported before but hasn’t been fixed, which is concerning given the risk it poses to vehicles. These potholes need urgent attention, or at the very least should be clearly marked, as they’re likely to cause further damage to other road users. I really hope this issue will now be taken as seriously as it should be, as it presents an ongoing hazard to the local area and people using this road.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 249, Oakleigh Road North, Brunswick Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Barbed wire hanging down clearly put there by house owner, 6th April
item.description:
It’s been here for months possibly years Exposed not secure barbed wire just hanging down It’s going to hurt a child or animals shortly It’s unacceptable and very dangerous It’s just as you walk onto the close around the bend and on right hand side hanging down exposed

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Tramway Close, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Blown bulb on streetlight, 6th April
item.description:
The bulb is blown on the streetlight which leaves the road in darkness
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 113, Albany Road, Blendon, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly Tipping, 6th April
item.description:
Some muppet has dumped a load of rubbish and a mattress here. Bags of smaller rubbish too. Looks likely to me that there might be an address in the smaller bags.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Thorntree Road, Charlton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 2 bikes chained to bike stand for 1-2 months, 6th April
item.description:
The bike stand outside Sainsburys has 2 abandoned bikes chained to it for 1-2 months. I use the bike stand regularly whenever I go to the store and the bikes have not moved in all this time . They take up space for other users including myself.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 158 - 162, High Street, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fall on footpath, 6th April
item.description:
I fell in West Way on 9 March 2026 having tripped on this uneven pathway. Apparently a neighbour fell a week before me in the same place, outside number 9. I have commented in the past about West Way where I have resided for 40 years, I feel it’s a forgotten road, the footpaths have many broken slabs, it’s a well used road for pedestrians up to Station Square. I am in my late 70’s and was lucky I did not break any bones. I look forward to your comments. I have informed Mr Fawthrop at the AGM,

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): West Way, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Hazardous Giant Hogweed growing along riverside path, 6th April
item.description:
The sap of the giant hogweed plant is phototoxic. The sap contains furanocoumarins, which leads to phytophotodermatitis, a serious skin inflammation.[43] A phototoxic reaction can begin as soon as 15 minutes after contact with the sap.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Homerton Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tree Preservation Order application, 6th April
item.description:
TPO application for tree in garden of 77 Portmeadow Walk, SE2 9UN

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Seacourt Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pile of burnt rubbish and dead fox, 6th April
item.description:
There is a pile of burnt rubbish and a dead fox on top of it.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Theydon Road, Hackney, London

item.title: England flag still not removed, 6th April
item.description:
I reported this exact issue in November and nothings been done. The reference was 8433065. It’s not safe for me to remove this myself, please give me an ETA thank you.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 8, Kent Road, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
